D9 Biology L4 Analyze Reference

Dinoflagellate Glow — Mechanical Stress → Blue Flash

Noctiluca and Pyrocystis dinoflagellates emit a single bright flash when sheared; the flash propagates along action-potential lines through the cell, fading in ~150 ms

A swimming fish or breaking wave produces shear stress that opens scintillon membrane proton channels; protonation activates luciferin (a tetrapyrrole) and luciferase, emitting at 470 nm blue. The flash is so brief (~100 ms) and bright (10⁸ photons) that the ocean lights up where it moves — the "milky sea" effect.

Drag mechanical stress. Below threshold no flash; above it the flash brightness saturates quickly. Time-trace shows the millisecond flash profile.
